description: DemoCorp Secure Engineering Policy — DPDP Act 2023 Compliant

rules.description: AWS and cloud provider credentials must never exist outside secrets vault. Any access key, secret key, or session token is a CRITICAL violation.
Database connection strings containing credentials are strictly forbidden. This includes PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, Redis URIs with embedded passwords.
Private cryptographic keys including RSA, EC, SSH, and PGP private keys are strictly prohibited from being copied or transmitted.
Internal microservice URLs, API gateway endpoints, and internal network addresses must not be shared externally.
Aadhaar numbers, PAN cards, and any Indian citizen PII are governed under DPDP Act 2023 and must be masked before sharing.
JWT tokens, OAuth bearer tokens, and session cookies contain active authentication state and must never be copied.
Production environment variables and .env file contents containing secrets must never leave the secure development environment.
Hardcoded passwords in any form — function arguments, config dictionaries, or string literals — violate secure coding standards.
